description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver encased in a rectangular wooden cabinet with a rich, warm brown finish and visible wood grain. The front panel features a large, cream-colored speaker grille above a black control panel, which is adorned with two prominent round knobs and a horizontal frequency display with white markings. Below the display, a row of white push buttons is present, likely for band or function selection. The sides of the cabinet each have a small, rectangular vent with horizontal slats, suggesting additional speaker or ventilation openings. The back panel is mostly flat with a large, dark perforated section for heat dissipation and access to internal components, as well as some printed technical information. The overall design is characteristic of mid-20th-century radios, blending functional controls with decorative woodwork.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a front speaker grille, two large knobs, a frequency display, and side vents. The cabinet is finished in warm brown wood with a classic mid-century design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the speaker grille, two large knobs, frequency display, and a row of white push buttons.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the wood grain and a rectangular vent with horizontal slats.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a large perforated panel for ventilation and technical markings.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with wood grain and a matching vent.
